title: Phenix Trading Pty Ltd — Environmental impact
investigation_objective: The Clean Energy Regulator permanently suspended Phenix Trading Pty Ltd's registration under the Renewable Energy (Electricity) Act 2000 on 5 March 2026, no longer satisfied it was a 'fit and proper person', following NSW IPART cancelling its Energy Savings Scheme/Peak Demand Reduction Scheme accreditation and rejecting approximately 618,555 Energy Savings Certificates worth an estimated $10 million over exaggerated freezer energy-efficiency claims and false information, and Victoria's Essential Services Commission separately suspending it (via subsidiary LEDSaves) from the Victorian Energy Upgrades scheme over falsely claimed weather-sealing and shower-rose installations.
scope: Registration permanently suspended by the Clean Energy Regulator on fit-and-proper-person grounds; NSW IPART rejected approximately 618,555 energy-efficiency certificates worth an estimated $10 million linked to the company; Separately suspended by Victoria's Essential Services Commission (via a subsidiary) from a state energy-upgrades scheme
